zotero.abstractNote: Learning the structure of Bayesian networks from observational data is a fundamental challenge in machine learning. While score-based structure learning is often formulated as a combinatorial optimization problem, recent advances in continuous constrained optimization have enabled the use of gradient-based methods. However, these approaches typically focus on finding a single maximum a posteriori (MAP) graph, failing to capture the uncertainty inherent in the structure learning process. In this work, we propose Differentiable Bayesian Structure Learning (DiBS), a framework for Bayesian structure learning that leverages differentiable optimization to perform posterior inference over the space of directed acyclic graphs (DAGs). DiBS defines a continuous latent space of graphs and learns a posterior distribution over this space using particle-based variational inference. We demonstrate that DiBS is scalable, achieves state-of-the-art performance in terms of posterior approximation quality, and provides well-calibrated uncertainty estimates.
